name: SEC EDGAR
description: Official US Securities and Exchange Commission filings database — every 10-K (annual report), 10-Q (quarterly), 8-K (material events), S-1 (IPO), Form 4 (insider trades), 13F (institutional holdings), and more. Free, official, no API key needed — but the SEC requires a contact email in the User-Agent header per their fair-use policy (https://www.sec.gov/os/accessing-edgar-data). Set your name + email on connect; we'll bake it into the UA. Form 4 (insider transactions) is consistently one of the most reliably alpha-generating stock signals: executives buying their own shares with cash predicts outperformance over the next 6 months at a statistically significant rate.

credential_fields.name: contact_name
credential_fields.label: Contact name / organization
credential_fields.description: Your name or company name. SEC asks who's calling — they reserve the right to block requests with generic User-Agents.

credential_fields.name: contact_email
credential_fields.label: Contact email
credential_fields.description: Your email. SEC includes this in their access logs and will email you if they need to throttle or block your traffic. They state explicitly that they don't share it.

tools.name: ticker_to_cik
tools.description: Resolve a stock ticker to its 10-digit zero-padded SEC CIK (Central Index Key). EVERY other EDGAR call needs a CIK, not a ticker — call this first to look up. The endpoint returns a JSON map of all ~10k SEC-registered companies; the agent can grep for the ticker. Cache aggressively: this file updates daily but doesn't move per request.

tools.name: company_submissions
tools.description: All recent filings for a company. Returns a 'filings.recent' object with parallel arrays: accessionNumber, filingDate, reportDate, form (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, 4, etc.), and primaryDocument. Use this to find what a company has filed lately — earnings reports, material events, insider trades, etc. CIK must be 10 digits, zero-padded (e.g. '0000320193' for Apple, not '320193').

cik.description: 10-digit zero-padded CIK. Get it from ticker_to_cik.

tools.name: company_facts
tools.description: All XBRL-tagged financial data for a company — every reported fundamental across every filing. Returns nested 'facts.us-gaap' (and 'facts.dei') with concepts like Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, Assets, Liabilities, OperatingIncomeLoss, etc., each holding a time series of reported values across filings. Massive payload (often >5MB for big issuers); use 'concept' instead when you only need one metric.

tools.name: concept
tools.description: Time series for ONE XBRL concept on ONE company. Use when you know exactly what you want — Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, EarningsPerShareBasic, CommonStockSharesOutstanding, etc. Smaller payload than company_facts. Taxonomy is almost always us-gaap; dei holds entity-level metadata.

tag.description: Concept name in CamelCase — Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, EarningsPerShareBasic, Assets, Liabilities, CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue, etc.

tools.name: concept_frame
tools.description: Cross-section: one XBRL concept across EVERY US public company for a single quarter or year. Use when ranking companies by a metric ('all companies that reported Q1 2026 revenue > $10B') or doing market-wide screens. Frame strings: 'CY2025Q1I' (point-in-time end-Q1-2025), 'CY2025Q1' (cumulative Q1-2025 flow), 'CY2025' (annual).

frame.description: CY2025Q1I = instant; CY2025Q1 = quarterly flow; CY2025 = annual.
